channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Chanel event brings Oscar nominees together the night before the big show
item.description: BEVERLY HILLS, Calif. (AP) — The patio of the storied Polo Lounge at the Beverly Hills Hotel was packed to the brim with Hollywood stars for the annual Chanel and Charles Finch dinner on Saturday, the final event before the 97th Academy Awards.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pope stable and resting in hospital, again skips Sunday noon blessing as he recovers from pneumonia
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis was in stable condition and resting on Sunday as he continued his recovery from double pneumonia, but again skipped his weekly noon blessing to avoid even a brief public appearance.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A topsy-turvy Oscar season concludes Sunday. Here's what to look for at Sunday's show
item.description: Sunday’s Academy Awards, at the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles, will bring to a close to one of the most unpredictable Oscar races in recent memory. The ceremony kicks off 7 p.m. EST and will be broadcast by ABC and streamed on Hulu. Conan O’Brien is hosting for the first time.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: About 8 in 10 say they've recently experienced extreme weather: Survey
item.description: Overall, nearly 70 percent of adults believe in climate change, with 93 percent of Democrats and 51 percent of Republicans saying so, poll results show.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE structure, authority emerge as biggest DC mystery under Trump
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency’s (DOGE) structure and authority has become a central mystery in the barrage of lawsuits seeking to stymie its operations.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Singer Angie Stone killed in car crash after leaving Alabama performance
item.description: Stone was riding in the back of a vehicle that "flipped over and subsequently hit by a big rig," according to family and friends.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge blocks Trump order threatening funding for institutions that offer care for transgender youth
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — President Donald Trump’s plan to pull federal funding from institutions that provide gender-affirming care for transgender youth will remain blocked on a long-term basis under a federal judge's ruling in Seattle late Friday. U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Juilliard-trained pianist convicted of murder after mid-trial confession leads to additional arrest
item.description: A South Carolina man was sentenced to life behind bars for murder after his confession on the stand led to an additional arrest in Christina Parcell's killing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Is there a secret behind the Netflix intro sound? Some viewers seem to think so
item.description: The origin of the familiar sound has become a topic of conversation among online conspiracy theorists in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Cuomo jumps into New York mayor's race, attempting political comeback
item.description: Former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo (D) announced Saturday afternoon that he's entering New York City’s mayoral race, adding a big name to the already busy Democratic primary challenging Mayor Eric Adams (D).
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US kills senior military leader of Al-Qaeda affiliate in Syria
item.description: U.S. Central Command announced on Saturday that it had killed a senior military leader of an Al-Qaeda affiliate known as Hurras al-Din in an airstrike in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FedEx plane makes emergency landing after bird strike
item.description: “FedEx Flight 3609 from Newark to Indianapolis experienced a bird strike during takeoff," a FedEx spokesperson said.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Black graves are being moved to make way for an industrial park, drawing a mix of emotions
item.description: DANVILLE, Va. (AP) — A decision to move the remains of hundreds African American tenant farmers from a former Virginia tobacco plantation to a dedicated burial ground has elicited a range of emotions among the sharecroppers’ descendants.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Alabama governor commutes death row inmate Rocky Myers' sentence to life in prison
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Alabama Gov. Kay Ivey commuted the death sentence of Robin “Rocky” Myers to life in prison Friday, saying there were enough questions about his guilt that she could not move forward with his execution.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Iowa's governor signs a bill removing gender identity protections from the state’s civil rights code
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Iowa became the first U.S. state to remove gender identity protections from its civil rights code on Friday when Gov. Kim Reynolds signed into law a bill that opponents say will expose transgender people and other Iowans to discrimination in all aspects of daily life.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Peak bloom for the iconic cherry blossom trees of Washington, DC, is predicted for late March
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For decades, the thousands of blooming cherry blossom trees in the nation’s capital have served as both a magnet to visitors and the unofficial start of the tourist season for Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Zelenskyy thanks Trump for visit after Oval Office clash
item.description: The meeting escalated after Vice President JD Vance suggested a diplomatic solution could be reached with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans target SNAP in federal spending cuts; food banks brace for impact
item.description: As House Republicans push to cut billions from the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP), commonly known as food stamps, food banks are bracing for potential impacts.
item.dc:creator: Raquel Martin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Landlord guilty on all counts in hate crime attack that killed Palestinian American boy, wounded his mother
item.description: Joseph Czuba, 73, faces first-degree murder, attempted murder and hate crime charges in the October 2023 attack that killed Wadee al-Fayoumi, and wounded his mother, Hanan Shaheen.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, NewsNation

item.title: Patrolling America's 'third border' from the skies
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard is taking to the skies to monitor Florida's coast as a crackdown on illegal immigration continues.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Tate brothers, who face human trafficking charges in Romania, are back in the US: What to know
item.description: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate, who are charged with human trafficking in Romania, arrived Thursday in the U.S. after authorities lifted travel restrictions on the siblings, who have millions of online followers.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Microsoft shutting down Skype in May
item.description: Microsoft is closing down Skype, the video-calling service it bought for $8.5 billion in 2011, which had helped spark a transformation in how people communicate online.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Police kill man suspected in Platte County killings, kidnapping
item.description: The Platte County Sheriff’s Office and the Riverside Missouri Police Department are investigating a double homicide and child abduction.
item.dc:creator: Hannah King, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Leonard Peltier remains defiant in AP interview, maintaining innocence and vowing continued activism
item.description: BELCOURT, N.D. (AP) — More than 50 years after a shootout on the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation landed him in federal prison, Leonard Peltier remains defiant.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Watch the full Trump-Zelenskyy Oval Office meeting
item.description: President Donald Trump shouted at Ukraine's leader on Friday during an extraordinary meeting in the Oval Office, berating President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for “gambling with millions of lives” and suggesting his actions could trigger World War III.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pope had bronchial spasm, resulted in him inhaling vomit, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis suffered a bronchial spasm on Friday that resulted in him breathing in vomit, requiring non-invasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said in relaying a setback in his two-week long battle against double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Musicians, bus driver caught with 200 pounds of cocaine
item.description: Officers sent the bus to secondary inspection after attempting to cross the Hidalgo-Reynosa International Bridge, where the discovery was made.
item.dc:creator: Dave Hendricks, NewsNation

item.title: Where things stand as Congress tries to avoid a partial government shutdown in two weeks
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As House members finished voting for the week and left Washington, the lead Democrat on the House Appropriations Committee, Rep.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: WATCH: Zelenskyy's White House meeting with Trump, Vance turns to shouting match
item.description: A White House meeting that was intended to kick off negotiations over a deal that would offer the United States access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals unraveled Friday afternoon as Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy clashed with President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance in the Oval Of
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, ZEKE MILLER and JUSTIN SPIKE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Law school project finds slavery citations still being used today
item.description: EAST LANSING, Mich. (AP) — An 1842 U.S. Supreme Court ruling overturning the kidnapping conviction of a white man who seized a Black family and forced them into slavery south of the Mason-Dixon line is still being cited in American jurisprudence, 160 years after enslaved people throughout the U.S.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: I-40 to reopen at Tennessee-North Carolina state line
item.description: The four-mile stretch of I-40 on the Tennessee and North Carolina border will reopen to motor traffic Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Naomi Hillmer, NewsNation

item.title: Skype shutting down in May as Microsoft pivots to Teams as alternative
item.description: (KRON) -- Skype will be shutting down after two decades, the internet-based phone and video platform announced on Friday. Skype will be going dark in May with parent company Microsoft looking to driver users to Microsoft Teams as an alternative.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Washington man arrested for stealing $8M boat with crew on board
item.description: The suspect allegedly tried to steal a different boat earlier that morning, but its crew "ran him off the boat," police said.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, NewsNation

item.title: Gene Hackman, wife tested negative for carbon monoxide, sheriff says
item.description: Actor Gene Hackman, his wife Betsy Arakawa, and their dog were found dead in their Santa Fe home, in a scene that one detective had deemed "suspicious enough in nature to require a thorough search and investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fallon Fischer, KRON4

item.title: A stretch of a North Carolina highway that collapsed during Helene is about to reopen
item.description: A stretch of Interstate 40 through the western North Carolina mountains is reopening to traffic this weekend, months after Hurricane Helene's historic flooding collapsed portions of the road, partially restoring the major travel connection with eastern Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he plans to add White House ballroom, pave part of the Rose Garden
item.description: President Trump in a new interview said he is planning to add a ballroom to the White House and to pave part the Rose Garden, similar to a patio at his Mar-a-Lago property in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: MAP: Listeria strain in deadly outbreak found in these states as death toll climbs
item.description: A listeria outbreak tied to supplement shakes is now linked to at least 12 deaths and 38 cases across 21 states, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5
